reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: worker.js runs the REAL transformers.js automatic-speech-recognition pipeline on `onnx-community/moonshine-base-ONNX` via the shared loadPipeline() helper (dtype q8), then `pipe(audio)` transcribes the whole short clip in one pass (no 30 s padding — Moonshine's compute scales with audio length). The transcript, token count, and real-time factor are all taken from the pipeline output + measured performance.now(). Conformance calls-real-inference (source pattern) + io-shape-semantic (transcript matches spoken audio) cover it. Code-grounded backfill; the page itself measures + reports real ms/rtf/speedup.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker; the 16 kHz mono Float32 samples are posted to the worker and the plain-text transcript comes back.

rubric.evidence: Input = a 16 kHz mono Float32Array (Web Audio decode + resample). Output = out.text (a plain transcript). Moonshine has no timestamp tokens, so the page correctly returns plain text and headlines the SPEED (real-time factor = process time ÷ audio length, and the derived speed-up). The empty case honestly shows '(no speech detected)'.
rubric.notes: The absence of timestamps is documented as expected behaviour (built for fast short-form ASR), not a bug.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/moonshine-base-ONNX, linked; Useful Sensors Moonshine base), the task, quantisation (q8, the *_quantized.onnx weights), backend (WASM; WebGPU when a real adapter exists), download (~65 MB), and license (MIT). ensureLoaded prefers WebGPU when navigator.gpu.requestAdapter() succeeds and falls back to WASM honestly, reporting device. Conformance declares-model-id/task/quantisation(q8)/backend covered.
rubric.notes: Backend reported in the readout (rBackend) is the one that actually loaded.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader ({ modelId: 'onnx-community/moonshine-base-ONNX', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:65, requiresWebGPU:false }); a valid current cached model auto-initialises with no Load click; onReady enables the mic/upload/Transcribe controls. Conformance shared-auto-init-loader + auto-init-on-cached cover it.
rubric.notes: Small ~65 MB model — quick to cache and re-init.

rubric.evidence: #status is role=status aria-live=polite; #transcript is aria-live. Failures surface: 'Couldn't decode that audio: …', 'Transcription failed: …' (.err), and a #micFallback panel when MicRecorder.supported() is false or getUserMedia is denied. Download progress flows from the pipeline progress_callback via {type:'progress'}. The shared loader exposes retry/recovery.
rubric.notes: No WebGPU required for the WASM default path; runs everywhere.

rubric.evidence: All controls real + keyboard-operable: Record (mic, user-initiated), Upload audio (file input), two sample chips (JFK, TED), the Transcribe button, and native <audio controls>. Mic capture requires a click and shows an explicit fallback when unavailable. buttons-have-names passes.
No canned transcript — text comes from the real pipeline; tokens, rtf, and speedup are measured on-device. Conformance no-fake-output (manual-evidenced) holds.

Every button has an accessible name; the waveform canvas carries aria-label='Audio waveform'; transcript + status are aria-live; the speed readout is text. Light+dark WCAG-AA design system. buttons-have-names + live-status-region conformance pass.

.audio-row/.sample-row flex-wrap, .wave inline-size:100%, readout wraps. responsive-desktop/responsive-mobile conformance assert no horizontal overflow. models.json support is needs-review with an automated note flagging sub-24px targets (desktop 9, mobile 4).
rubric.notes: HONEST WEAKNESS: sub-24px tap targets flagged (sample chips + native audio controls). Follow-up matrix pass should raise chip tap size and flip support to ok.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in the Web Worker; the main thread only decodes audio and draws the bounded waveform. Moonshine's whole value is latency, which the page measures honestly (rtf + Nx real-time badge). Conformance inference-off-main-thread passes.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — the transcribe call never blocks the interface.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work off main thread web worker; ASR without blocking UI
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — transcription runs in a dedicated module Web Worker; only audio decode + a bounded waveform draw run on the main thread.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive audio control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .audio-row/.sample-row flex-wrap; canvas inline-size:100%. Follow-up: raise sub-24px chip tap targets.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-desktop/responsive-mobile assert no-horizontal-overflow; support needs-review for tap size

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status + transcript announcement; media element accessibility
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error + live transcript via role=status/aria-live; give every control an accessible name; use native <audio controls>.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — #status role=status aria-live; #transcript aria-live; native <audio controls>; labelled buttons.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible error announcement for mic unavailable / decode failure
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Surface errors to the user in an accessible, live-announced way rather than only in the console.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — decode/transcription failures set an .err status in the aria-live region; #micFallback explains an unavailable microphone with a sample/upload alternative.

openQuestions: The 'wild' use case is real-time streaming captions in a rolling loop — does the current single-shot worker call cleanly extend to a continuous mic loop without dropped chunks on WASM?
How many of the flagged sub-24px targets are the native <audio> controls (outside our styling) versus sample chips we can enlarge?

followUpGoals.goal: Run the mobile+desktop responsive matrix, raise sample-chip tap targets to ≈44px, and flip models.json support from needs-review to ok.
Prototype a live-streaming captions surface that chunks the mic via AudioWorklet and runs Moonshine in a rolling loop, exercising its low-latency short-form strength.

summary: Retrospective code-grounded critique backfill for moonshine-asr (onnx-community/moonshine-base-ONNX, ASR, q8, ~65 MB, MIT, WASM default). REAL inference: the worker runs the transformers.js ASR pipeline via the shared loadPipeline helper, one pass over the whole short clip (no 30 s padding), off the main thread, and reports the real-time factor + speed-up it measured. Controls (mic record user-initiated + fallback, upload, samples, transcribe, audio) are all real; the transcript is the real output; no timestamps is documented as expected. Auto-init via createModelLoader; honest WASM/WebGPU handling. HONEST WEAKNESS: automated pre-scan flagged sub-24px tap targets (9 desktop / 4 mobile), support needs-review — a follow-up matrix pass should raise chip tap sizes. guidanceConsulted: performance, css-layout, accessibility, accessible-error-announcement. Grounded in code + the immutable conformance suite; no measured numbers fabricated.
